questions.explanation: Rest gathers all args into an array; reduce sums them.
Rest must come LAST — it gathers everything left over.

questions.prompt: Compared to a regular function, an arrow function:
questions.explanation: Lexical `this` — arrows don't rebind. That's why they're perfect inside callbacks.

questions.code:
const fn = (a, b = a) => b;
console.log(fn(5));

options: An anonymous arrow expression passed as callback
questions.explanation: Functions are values. Passing one as an argument is a callback.
Closures see the CURRENT value of variables in their scope — not a snapshot.
Wrap an object literal in () to return it implicitly from an arrow.
